sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचअभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः । दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले  भयका सर्वथा अभाव अन्तःकरणकी शुद्धि ज्ञानके लिये योगमें दृढ़ स्थिति सात्त्विक दान इन्द्रियोंका दमन यज्ञ स्वाध्याय कर्तव्यपालनके लिये कष्ट सहना शरीरमनवाणीकी सरलता ॥
englishTranslation: Fearlessness, purity of mind, steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge, generosity, self-control, sacrifice, study of the sacred texts, austerity, and straightforwardness.
essence: Right action starts with disciplined character.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A divine life begins with fearlessness, inner purity, self-control, generosity, sacrifice, study, disciplined effort, and honesty.
simpleInsight: A strong character is built, not wished into existence.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Krishna begins listing the qualities that belong to a life shaped by the divine. Arjuna needs more than courage to fight; he needs a character strong enough to act rightly under pressure.
modernRelevance: You are about to send the message, walk into the meeting, or tell the truth that may change everything. Before the outcome, the verse asks for steadiness, honesty, and self-mastery.
translationLiteral: अभयम् (fearlessness) / सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः (purity of being) / ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः (steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge) / दानम् (giving) / दमः (self-control) / च (and) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / च (and) / स्वाध्यायः (study of sacred texts) / तपः (austerity) / आर्जवम् (straightforwardness)
